info.description: QuiverAI-compatible API clone for local development and self-hosting.
info.title: Quiver Clone API

tags.description: Operations for listing and retrieving available models.
tags.name: Models

tags.description: Generate SVG graphics from text prompts and reference images.
tags.name: Create SVGs

tags.description: Convert raster image inputs into SVG outputs.
tags.name: Vectorize SVG

BearerAuth.description: Authentication via Bearer token API key

svg_generate.description: Deprecated — use `pricing_credits`. Legacy USD price string per SVG generation request.

svg_vectorize.description: Deprecated — use `pricing_credits`. Legacy USD price string per SVG vectorization request.

pricing.description: Deprecated. Prefer `pricing_credits` for per-request credit debits. USD strings are legacy placeholders.

svg_generate.description: Credits debited from the organization balance per SVG generation request for this model.

svg_vectorize.description: Credits debited from the organization balance per SVG vectorization request for this model.

credits.description: Credit cost for this request. Use this for billing instead of `usage` tokens.

input_tokens.description: Deprecated. Token counts are retained for compatibility and may be zeroed.

output_tokens.description: Deprecated. Token counts are retained for compatibility and may be zeroed.

total_tokens.description: Deprecated. Token counts are retained for compatibility and may be zeroed.

event.description: The SSE event name (sent via the `event:` line).

id.description: Optional SSE event id (sent via the `id:` line).

retry.description: Optional SSE retry value in milliseconds (sent via the `retry:` line).

event.description: The SSE event name (sent via the `event:` line).

id.description: Optional SSE event id (sent via the `id:` line).

retry.description: Optional SSE retry value in milliseconds (sent via the `retry:` line).

event.description: The SSE event name (sent via the `event:` line).

id.description: Optional SSE event id (sent via the `id:` line).

retry.description: Optional SSE retry value in milliseconds (sent via the `retry:` line).

event.description: The SSE event name (sent via the `event:` line).

id.description: Optional SSE event id (sent via the `id:` line).

retry.description: Optional SSE retry value in milliseconds (sent via the `retry:` line).

event.description: The SSE event name (sent via the `event:` line).

id.description: Optional SSE event id (sent via the `id:` line).

retry.description: Optional SSE retry value in milliseconds (sent via the `retry:` line).

SvgStreamEvent.description: Server-sent event (SSE) envelope for SVG streaming operations. Each SSE message uses the `event:` line for the phase discriminator and the `data:` line for a JSON payload. For `n > 1`, events are interleaved: use `data.index` for output position and `data.id` as the stable per-output identifier. Terminal failures emit `event: error` with public error data. The stream terminates with `data: [DONE]`.

SvgStreamEventData.description: The event payload. Shape depends on the `type` phase discriminator.

id.description: A unique identifier for the SVG output when available. Stable per output across streamed events.

index.description: Zero-based output index for this event. Present for multi-output streams (`n > 1`).

svg.description: Optional SVG payload for forward compatibility.

text.description: Optional generation status or reasoning text.

id.description: A unique identifier for the SVG output when available. Stable per output across streamed events.

index.description: Zero-based output index for this event. Present for multi-output streams (`n > 1`).

svg.description: Optional SVG payload for forward compatibility.

text.description: Optional reasoning text (present on reasoning events for some operations).

id.description: A unique identifier for the SVG output. For streaming multi-output requests (`n > 1`), each output has a distinct `id` and all events for that output reuse that same `id`.

index.description: Zero-based output index for this event. Present for multi-output streams (`n > 1`).

svg.description: The SVG markup (partial during draft, complete during content).

text.description: Optional reasoning text (present on reasoning events for some operations).

credits.description: Credit cost for this completed SVG output. Emitted on `content` events.

id.description: A unique identifier for the SVG output. For streaming multi-output requests (`n > 1`), each output has a distinct `id` and all events for that output reuse that same `id`.

index.description: Zero-based output index for this event. Present for multi-output streams (`n > 1`).

svg.description: The SVG markup (partial during draft, complete during content).

text.description: Optional reasoning text (present on reasoning events for some operations).

max_output_tokens.description: Upper bound for output token count.

model.description: Model identifier to use for generation or vectorization.

presence_penalty.description: Penalty for tokens already present in prior output.

stream.description: When true, emits a Server-Sent Events stream.

auto_crop.description: Auto-crop image to the dominant subject before vectorization.

height.description: Height of the SVG viewBox. Must be greater than zero.

minX.description: Minimum x coordinate of the SVG viewBox.

minY.description: Minimum y coordinate of the SVG viewBox.

width.description: Width of the SVG viewBox. Must be greater than zero.

SvgAttributes.description: Optional SVG root attributes requested by the caller.

ImageInputReference.description: Image input reference. Accepts a network image URL or a base64-encoded image payload. Decoded images must be no larger than 12582912 bytes, 4096x4096 pixels, or 16777216 total pixels. Accepted direct media types: image/png, image/jpeg, image/webp, image/gif, image/svg+xml.

url.description: Network image URL. Only http/https URLs are allowed. The API fetches the URL, follows up to 3 redirects, blocks private or reserved network targets, requires an image response content type, and applies the same decoded image limits as base64 inputs.

base64.description: Base64-encoded image payload. Decoded images must be no larger than 12582912 bytes, 4096x4096 pixels, or 16777216 total pixels. Accepted direct media types: image/png, image/jpeg, image/webp, image/gif, image/svg+xml.

max_output_tokens.description: Upper bound for output token count.

model.description: Model identifier to use for generation or vectorization.

presence_penalty.description: Penalty for tokens already present in prior output.

stream.description: When true, emits a Server-Sent Events stream.

instructions.example: Use a flat monochrome style with clean geometry.

prompt.description: Primary text prompt that describes the desired SVG.
prompt.example: Generate an icon of a unicorn

references.description: Optional reference images to guide style/composition. Accepts `{ url }`, `{ base64 }`, or URL string shorthand. Runtime limits are model-specific: 4 for Arrow 1.1/Arrow 1.x aliases, 16 for Arrow 1.1 Max.

anyOf.description: String shorthand for a network image URL. Equivalent to `{ "url": "..." }`. The same URL safety and decoded image limits apply.

get.description: Returns all models available to the authenticated organization.

X-Trace-ID.description: Client-supplied trace identifier echoed when the request includes `x-trace-id`.

X-Request-ID.description: Server-generated request identifier for tracing and support.

data.description: First-gen vector-native model for editable logos, icons, and illustrations.
data.name: Arrow 1.0

data.description: Best default for speed and quality, with strong prompt following and precise SVG.
data.name: Arrow 1.1

data.description: Higher-fidelity model for detailed diagrams and illustrations.
data.name: Arrow 1.1 Max

post.description: Converts an image input into an SVG output.

schema.description: Optional client-supplied trace identifier. The API echoes this value in `X-Trace-ID` and includes it in request logs for client-side correlation.

parameters.description: Optional client-supplied trace identifier. The API echoes this value in `X-Trace-ID` and includes it in request logs for client-side correlation.
parameters.name: x-trace-id

200.description: When `stream` is false, returns `application/json` with the full SVG response. When `stream` is true, returns `text/event-stream` with Server-Sent Events. Each SSE message contains an `event:` line (`generating`, `reasoning`, `draft`, or `content`) and a `data:` line with a JSON payload. `usage` token fields are deprecated and set to `0`; use `credits` for billing values. For streaming, `credits` is emitted on completed `content` events. The stream terminates with `data: [DONE]`.

X-RateLimit-Limit.description: Maximum number of requests allowed in the current rate-limit window.

X-RateLimit-Remaining.description: Remaining requests in the current rate-limit window.

X-RateLimit-Reset.description: Unix timestamp in milliseconds when the current rate-limit window resets.

X-Trace-ID.description: Client-supplied trace identifier echoed when the request includes `x-trace-id`.

X-Request-ID.description: Server-generated request identifier for tracing and support.

value.instructions: Use a flat monochrome style with rounded corners and clean geometry.
value.prompt: Generate a minimalist unicorn icon for a SaaS dashboard
Generate an icon of a unicorn

streamN2.summary: Streaming generation with two interleaved outputs

value.instructions: Use flat monochrome geometry and keep them visually distinct.
value.prompt: Generate two minimalist unicorn badge variants

200.description: When `stream` is false, returns `application/json` with the full SVG response. When `stream` is true, returns `text/event-stream` with Server-Sent Events. Each SSE message contains an `event:` line (`generating`, `reasoning`, `draft`, or `content`) and a `data:` line with a JSON payload. For `n > 1`, events are interleaved across outputs, each output keeps a stable `data.id`, and `data.index` indicates the output position. `usage` token fields are deprecated and set to `0`; use `credits` for billing values. For streaming, `credits` is emitted on completed `content` events. The stream terminates with `data: [DONE]`.

X-RateLimit-Limit.description: Maximum number of requests allowed in the current rate-limit window.

X-RateLimit-Remaining.description: Remaining requests in the current rate-limit window.

X-RateLimit-Reset.description: Unix timestamp in milliseconds when the current rate-limit window resets.

X-Trace-ID.description: Client-supplied trace identifier echoed when the request includes `x-trace-id`.

X-Request-ID.description: Server-generated request identifier for tracing and support.

n2ContentEvent.summary: Final content event for output index 1 (same output id)

n2DraftEvent.summary: Interleaved draft event for output index 1 (n=2)

X-Request-ID.description: Server-generated request identifier for tracing and support.

example.message: The request could not be completed. Please retry.
